player_turn_cases.description: Simple first-person action that stays within ability and authority limits.

pc.short_description: A human with standard normative abilities.

data.long_description:
A woman in her mid-30s living in Chicago, USA working as a project manager at a mid-sized marketing firm. Lives in a one-bedroom apartment in a mixed residential/commercial neighborhood and commutes by public transit.

Maintains a stable daily routine: commuting, attending meetings, managing timelines, and coordinating with colleagues. Travels for work roughly once a quarter for client meetings, team offsites, or conferences, typically for a few days at a time.

Balances work responsibilities with personal life, including errands, exercise, and leisure. Goes to a gym or fitness class a few times a week, and occasionally runs along the lakefront in warmer months. Enjoys low-effort hobbies such as watching TV series, listening to podcasts, and reading contemporary fiction. Socially, meets friends for dinner, drinks, or brunch on weekends and keeps in touch via group chats.

Keeps her apartment reasonably clean and organized, doing routine chores on weekends. Uses common tools like a phone calendar and reminders to manage schedules and travel. Recovers from stress through routine activities such as watching TV, exercising, or spending time with friends, and occasionally takes short trips or visits family a few times a year.
abilities: Can perform all average human abilities, with no significant impairments or exceptionalities.
Can not perform any superhuman abilities or feats of magic, strength, speed, intelligence, or perception.
goals: maintain steady career progression in project management
perform well on client-facing work and team coordination
maintain financial stability and savings
balance work responsibilities with personal time
stay physically active and maintain general health
maintain friendships through regular social interaction
travel occasionally for both work and leisure
keep living space manageable and comfortable
stay organized across work, travel, and daily responsibilities
recover from stress and avoid burnout

npc.short_description: A human with standard normative abilities.

data.long_description:
A man in his early 40s living in a mid-sized U.S. city working as a research biologist at a university-affiliated lab. Splits time between laboratory work, data analysis, and administrative responsibilities such as grant writing and supervising junior researchers.

Maintains a structured weekday routine: arriving at the lab in the morning, checking ongoing experiments, analyzing data, attending meetings, and coordinating with collaborators. Occasionally teaches or guest lectures. Travels a few times a year for academic conferences, fieldwork, or collaboration meetings.

Balances professional responsibilities with personal life. Lives in a modest house or apartment, maintains it with regular upkeep, and handles errands on evenings or weekends. Engages in hobbies such as reading scientific literature, light outdoor activities (e.g., hiking or birdwatching), and casual media consumption like documentaries or streaming series.

Socially, maintains a small but stable network of colleagues and friends. Interacts regularly at work and meets friends or peers occasionally for meals or informal gatherings. Keeps in touch with family and may visit them a few times a year.

Uses standard tools (calendar, email, task lists) to manage responsibilities. Handles stress through routine, structured problem-solving, exercise, or quiet leisure activities. Capable of adapting to schedule changes, grant deadlines, or experimental setbacks without significant disruption.
abilities: Can perform all average human abilities, with no significant impairments or exceptionalities.
Can not perform any superhuman abilities or feats of magic, strength, speed, intelligence, or perception.
goals: advance research projects and publish findings
secure funding through grants
maintain steady career progression in academia or industry
mentor junior researchers or students
maintain financial stability
balance work with personal time and rest
stay intellectually engaged in scientific developments
maintain physical health through moderate activity
keep living space functional and organized
manage long-term projects without burnout

player_turn_cases.transcript: Opening scene: You enter a new space. In this space, a closed supply room door sits at the end of a conference hallway, and a flashlight rests on a nearby table while NB stands beside the door.
player_turn_cases.player_action: I grab the flashlight and shine it under the door to see what is on the other side.
player_turn_cases.notes: Using a plausible nearby tool should pass when the player only attempts the action and does not author what they discover.

player_turn_cases.description: Instructional or adversarial meta input should fail because it is not an in-world first-person action.

simulator_turn_cases.simulator_response: The door creaks but does not open. NB steps back.

Goes to a gym or fitness class a few times a week, and occasionally runs along the lakefront in warmer months. Enjoys low-effort hobbies such as watching TV series, listening to podcasts, and reading contemporary fiction. Socially, meets friends for dinner, drinks, or brunch on weekends and keeps in touch via group chats.

Keeps her apartment reasonably clean and organized, doing routine chores on weekends. Uses common tools like a phone calendar and reminders to manage schedules and travel. Recovers from stress through routine activities such as watching TV, exercising, or spending time with friends, and occasionally takes short trips or visits family a few times a year.
